_doc: Tuning for the ambient vision sensor (ears/vision.py) and the on-demand eyes (eyes/look.py). Edit freely; face-engine and the eyes re-read this file on next invocation.

_model_options.yolov8n: nano  — ~6 MB, ~6 ms CPU. Recommended for always-on / low-power. Default.
_model_options.yolov8s: small — ~22 MB, ~12 ms CPU. Better small-object recall. Still laptop-friendly.
_model_options.yolov8m: medium — ~52 MB, ~30 ms CPU. Noticeable jump in accuracy. Needs a real GPU or patience.
_model_options.yolov8l: large — ~87 MB. For desktops with a dedicated GPU.
_model_options.yolov8x: xlarge — ~136 MB. Max accuracy. GPU only, slow on CPU.

_interval_note: How often ears/vision.py takes an ambient snap. 30s is a good always-on default. Drop to 5-10s if you want faster reactions; raise to 120s+ to save power. The sensor is multi-purpose (see purposes below), so it runs whenever the agent is awake.
_camera_note: Null = auto-detect (tests indices 0-4 by brightness). Set an integer to pin a specific camera if auto-detect picks the wrong one.
purposes: ambient_scene_snapshot — writes scene.json every interval so face-engine, brain, and any other process can read who/what is in view.
sleep_detection — long stretches with no people detected feed the face into sleep/droop modes.
keep_face_alive — gaze target shifts to detected people; face stays responsive even when the agent session is mid-compaction or idle.
trigger_active_vision — when something interesting appears the agent can call eyes/look.py for a fresh photo/video + Claude Vision analysis.
